content.full_text:
Farn Pouucal PnecrrcEs CoururssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Apil26,2005
Robert Brennan
P.O. Box 463
Arvin, CA 93203
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-05-079
Dear Mr. Brennan:
This correspondence confirms our receipt of your request for advice regarding
Councilmember Angie McNeill of the Arvin City Council. The Fair Political practices
Commission ("FPPC') has withdrawn your April 18, 2005 request for written advice under the
provisions of the Political Reform Act. As our question now relates to past conduct, we are
unable to provide you with a written response.
Further, while the Act requires that the Commission provide form written advice or
informal assistance to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person,s
authorized representative, the Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public
official's duties under the Act. (See section 83114; subdivision (b); regulation lB3Zg,
subdivision (bX8XA), copy enclosed.)
If you believe that a violation of the Act has occurred, you may file a complaint with the
Commission's Enforcement Division. I have enclosed brochures explaining the complaint
procedure and a complaint form. Please contact the Enforcement Division at (800) SOt-tSOt, if
you have any questions about the complaint procedure.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Councilmember Angie McNeill of the Arvin City Council complied with her duties under the Political Reform Act regarding past conduct.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for written advice because it relates to past conduct, and the FPPC does not provide advice to third parties about a public official's duties under the Act. The requestor may file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter dated April 26, 2005. No formal Question or Conclusion sections present; advice letter identifies the subject matter and explains withdrawal basis. Document clearly states FPPC withdrawal of the request and references applicable statute and regulation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember Angie McNeill of the Arvin City Council complied with her duties under the Political Reform Act regarding past conduct.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for written advice because it relates to past conduct, and the FPPC does not provide advice to third parties about a public official's duties under the Act. The requestor may file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation occurred.
